summary: Using `VIEW_INDEX` in shader causes crash with Vulkan mobile renderer

description.text:

- Reproducible in: v4.5.dev.custom_build [d59994688]

### System information

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `MeshInstance3D` with a `BoxMesh` shape
2. Add a new `ShaderMaterial` in its "Surface Material Override"
3. Add a new `Shader` which uses `VIEW_INDEX` at all
4. It doesn't matter if the multiview shaders are enabled or not (they are enabled in my MRP)

Here's an example shader:

```
shader_type spatial;

uniform vec3 albedo_left : source_color;
uniform vec3 albedo_right : source_color;

void fragment() {
	ALBEDO = int(VIEW_INDEX) == int(0) ? albedo_left : albedo_right;
}
```

summary: Tiles with size bigger than 1x1 in atlas rotate incorrectly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5dev4 and later
- Not reproducible in 4.5dev3 and earlier

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev5.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12400F (12 threads)

### Issue description

Most likely related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105861

I'm not sure if it's supposed to be like that and was just broken before, but it for sure corrupted our maps a little.

Before dev4 when rotating bigger tiles whole thing stayed in the same spot, now it's rotated around origin point of that tile as shown on gif's bellow.


| Before             |  Now |
:-------------------------:|:-------------------------:
![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13e2e41c-141f-4b49-9fe3-0f9d31759128)  |  ![](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2eda343c-147e-4e2b-a77c-e3cfa9f89e9a)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6384a874-a2d2-41f7-ad76-6a3a13bbf115) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8336d59c-eff0-4838-bceb-5c4fa892b410)


This small difference caused a lot of tiles getting moved on our map where rotated tiles were used. I'm really not sure if this is intended or not since on the other hand it works way better in different cases where size is not rectangular.
Reporting just in case it's actually not intended before i go through everything to fix it manually.
I assume making each rotation variant as separate alternative tile would be more correct approach?
Anyway it worked before so that's how we did it. Thanks

| Before             |  Now |
:-------------------------:|:-------------------------:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f57c3fd8-d94c-4b97-abc6-d351235594d5)  |  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/27500282-f5a2-428d-8bf0-14db767ad09d)

summary: Internal Nodes do not use Parent's material

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.stable.mono, 4.3.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21001.9024) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

Possibly related to #93320, which was fixed on #106746 but only for the `TabContainer` Node.

If a Node internally creates new Nodes as children, those children do not respect the parent's shader.

Here's an example to better explain what I mean:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/645cb1fa-e276-4e16-a107-6400c93d894d)

In this scene, the `ProgressBar` and `HSlider` Nodes were created manually. The scroll bars inside the `TextEdit` and `ScrollContainer` Nodes were created automatically and thus are not visible from the Editor. All Nodes share the same `Control` parent.

I wrote a shader to replace the magenta color from the UI with a dithering effect, and I applied this shader to the base `Control` Node. All children Nodes were then set to Use Parent Material. This was the result:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2d8bd97e-024c-4a63-bc72-d22484035e7b)

Every Node manually added to the Scene was affected correctly by the shader, but the internal Nodes that were created automatically by other Nodes were not. Applying the shader directly to those Nodes produce the same result. A comment left by [KoBeWi](https://github.com/KoBeWi) on #106746 mentioned this exact issue:

> I think other complex controls will have the same problem.

Well, they were right.


### Steps to reproduce

Create a new shader and apply it to a Node with internal Nodes (`ScrollContainer`, `TextEdit`, etc.). The shader will not be applied to those internal Nodes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Shader Error.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20612613/Shader.Error.zip)

### Issue description

During the export of Android build through gradle, the logs are being accumulated instead of being printed one by one - which leads to crashing of the godot process, if the amount of logs are exceeding the buffer size.

### Steps to reproduce

-Start a fresh Godot project
-Install Android Build template
-Enable the "Use Gradle Build"
-Add "org.gradle.logging.level=debug" to gradle.properties in android/build directory
-Launch the `godot -v --headless --export-debug Android example.apk` command


**Expected result:**
See the logs as they are generated
**Actual result:**
Logs are being displayed when the process have finished the exporting

summary: Physic Interpolation on moved hidden object lock them on place

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.dev5
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.dev4 and below

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev5 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21001.9024) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-13600KF (20 threads)

### Issue description

After having moved an hidden object (Here using a tween, I did not test with other type of motion) with physic interpolation on, if you show the object, it will lock it in place, and make it unable to move (at least with a tween, even if you kill and recreate the tween)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83e2b64a-ec83-44a9-9202-f6a4809ca61c

This only happen in 4.5.dev5

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a visible object A and hide it

I created a minimal WebXR project with the reference code from https://docs.godotengine.org/de/4.x/classes/class_webxrinterface.html

summary: Autoload UI makes it seem like you can only use scripts

description.text:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon (TM) R9 200 Series (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 27.20.1034.6) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

This is a UI/UX issue as opposed to a functionality issue. I recently learned when looking for how I could add export variables to an Autoload (singleton) that you can use scenes as singletons in addition to scripts, which allows for setting the export variables in the scene.

It is not only unclear from the current autoload UI that this is possible, but the "Add" button only allows adding scripts. To add a scene as an Autoload, you must first create the scene separately, and then input its path (or use the path selection button).

The only hint in the UI that this is possible is the fact that when clicking the folder selection button, it lists .tscn and related as possible extensions (also for some reason a ton of other extension which don't seem like they should be there). The docs on Autoload mention it can be a script or scene, but the UI explicitly only says script.

It's very frustrating that I didn't realize this was possible until I was searching for a specific problem that required it, and I think it should be made very clear it can be a scene, as well as give that option using the "Add" button. I only ever use "Add" for the Autoloads, so would never have even seen that *.tscn is a possible file selection.



### Steps to reproduce

Open "Project Settings", go to "Autoload" tab. The setting for "Path" explicitly says "Set path or press "Add" to create a script", which to me implies you can only set the path to a script. Furthermore, when pressing "Add", the only option is to add a script, not a scene.

I believe it should be called out explicitly in the Path entry it can be a scene (something like "Script or Scene: Set path or press "Add" to create new", which also ensures those with smaller screens will still see the "Script or Scene" part at the beginning). Then when pressing "Add" it should give 2 options in a drop-down, one for "Script" and one for "Scene".

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

I am currently using force_drag(), and it does not update the drag preview properly.

var item: Item = source_slot.item.duplicate()

	var item_preview_texture: TextureRect = TextureRect.new()

summary: Add `OS::open_with_program` for opening files/directories with a specific program on macOS

description.text:
Fixes #94173.

This adds a new method to `OS` called `open_with_program`, which by default just defers to `create_process`, but on macOS will use the `NSWorkspace` method `openURLs:withApplicationAtURL`, instead of using `openApplicationAtURL` and passing the paths as normal process arguments.

This allows the `filesystem/external_programs/*` editor settings (as used by the "Open in External Program" context menu option in FileSystem) to actually work on macOS, since programs like Photoshop or Affinity Photo will otherwise (with `OS::create_process`) just open up but not actually open the specified file.

A couple of things to note:

1. I omitted the process ID return value, since it didn't seem as relevant here, especially since you can end up reusing an already running process.
2. ~~I could not figure out how to implement this for macOS <10.15, so I've just deferred to `create_process`, which is likely not correct, but I don't know how to test this anyway.~~

Steam Runtime 2 (soldier) 2 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970 (nvidia) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7600K CPU @ 3.80GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

I'm not sure if this is the same issue as https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/44466 but I really do not think that this is desirable behaviour. Somehow it seems that the light energy in the sky is off from the energy in the foreground by a factor of PI?

```DirectionalLight3D``` in light and sky mode with 1.0 energy:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/543037f6-9c4b-445f-b7e8-251618dfc684)

You can remove these ugly silhouettes by using two ```DirectionalLight3D```s, one in sky only mode and one in light only mode, and then setting the sky only light's energy to the energy of the light only lights's multiplied by PI.

```DirectionalLight3D``` in light only mode with 1.0 energy + ```DirectionalLight3D``` in sky only mode with PI energy:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0051c949-ca08-4e62-ba4c-1bd27d865422)

### Steps to reproduce

- Add ```WorldEnvironment``` with a sky and non-volumetric fog which uses sun scatter.
- Add ```DirectionalLight3D``` in light and sky mode.
- Add mesh.
- Position camera so that mesh is partially / fully covered by fog and in front of sun scatter in sky.

summary: Unnecessary floating point digits being serialized in some (but not all) cases

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.dev5
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.dev4

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev5 - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 29 May 2025 21:42:00 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (RPL-P) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

The new floating point serialization from https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98750 has caused a few cases where numbers get serialized with unnecessary digits, causing git noise. Specifically, with shader uniforms (both global and local) and `@export` variables. It doesn't seem to affect the Environment resource.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a float global uniform in the project settings
2. Set the value to something else, e.g. 0.02 or 1.2
---
1. Create a script
2. Add a float property with `@export_range(0, 2, 0.1)` and a default value of 1
3. Add a float property with `@export` and a default value of 1
4. Use the slider to change both values to 1.2
5. In the .tscn, the first one will be 1.2000000000000002, and the second will be 1.1999999999970896
---
1. Add a WorldEnvironment
2. Change a value (for example, the tonemap exposure) to 1.2
3. Check the .tscn file and see that it has been serialized as 1.2 without any unnecessary digits

### Issue description

Git plugin GDExtension fails to load when using **Project > Reload Current Project** due to Windows file locking limitations. This is not an issue when starting the editor through other means; only *restarting* the editor immediately after it's been shut down causes this issue.

summary: Make `BaseButton`s' shortcut feedback timers internal

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

When a `BaseButton`'s `shortcut_feedback` property is enabled and it is activated by a shortcut, a `Timer` is added as the button's child. However, this timer is not made internal. This PR makes the timer internal to avoid unexpected behavior when getting a button's children.

summary: Unique instance shader parameters from different shaders assigned to the same MeshInstance3D affect each other's values

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproduced on Windows 11 in: 
- `v4.5.dev5.mono.official [64b09905c]` 
- `v4.5.dev4.mono.official [209a446e3]`
- `v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]`


### System information

Godot v4.5.dev5.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

If a mesh has more than one `ShaderMaterial` using different `VisualShader`s that each have uniquely named instance shader parameters, modifying one of the instance shader parameter values either via the inspector or through code (tested in C# using `GeometryInstance3D.SetInstanceShaderParameter` method) will modify the instance shader parameters on the other material assigned to the same `MeshInstance3D`.  The values do not change in the inspector but the visual outcome is changed as if they were.  Even parameters of different types seam to modify each other's data.  This happens in the editor and while playing.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/50332c01-7727-4661-b712-35031f831723)

If a single material with multiple instance shader parameters is assigned to all material slots of a `MeshInstance3D` or if multiple materials using the same shader are applied to a single `MeshInstance3D`, then the instance parameters appear to behave correctly.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d907fdad-d36d-4a6e-a4ca-2e5842967af6)

In some cases, a parameter may not be affected by this issue though I haven't been able to nail down a rhyme or reason as to reproducing that case.



### Steps to reproduce

**Using repro project**: 
- Open `Cube_With_Multiple_Materials.tscn` (or add `Cube_With_Multiple_Materials.glb` to a new scene).
- Select the "Cube" `MeshInstance3D` and adjust the value of either of the 2 instance shader parameters.

**Repro from scratch**
- Create 2 unique `Spatial` `VisualShader`s each with their own uniquely named `FloatParameter` with `Qualifier` set to `Instance` and connect them to something visually obvious (mix node between two colors for example).
- Create `ShaderMaterial`s using each of the above shaders.
- Apply those materials to a single mesh with multiple material slots.
- Adjust the value of either of the instance shader parameters on the `MeshInstance3D`.

[shader-instance-property-issue-repro-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20561847/shader-instance-property-issue-repro-project.zip)

summary: Irrelevant 3D lights are chosen per mesh in Mobile and Compatibility renderers

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 and 4.5-dev4

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045), NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 Ti, Core(TM) i5-10300H CPU @ 2.50GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

When using the Compatibility (and also the Mobile) renderer I was expecting to have up to 8-Omni and 8-Spot lights render on each mesh.  Designing the scene to have more small meshes, and respecting the total light limit, I still had meshes that looked unlit.  In the following procedural office scene there are 28 Omnilights (close to the floor so it's easy to see where they are) and one Spotlight and 1 directional light.  Each room is its own mesh and has at least 1 light, and the yellow rooms have 3x2 and 2x2 lights.  You can see where lights were not rendering as you might expect:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5e29a868-34e8-400c-b55e-aacc4fede71f)

Digging into the code, I found that the Compatibility and Mobile renderers both have a function named **pair_light_instances**, and in that function only the first N lights that pass the culling stage are linked.  I changed the code to keep only the closest N lights and it rendered as expected.  Then I tried changing the project settings to allow 256 lights total, with 16 per mesh and tried minimal test project, but still some meshes picked weird lights:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a93c8c9f-4e0b-4e4a-a30a-d5d1df593f0b)

I then found that **RendererSceneCull::_scene_cull** has a fixed upper limit of **MAX_INSTANCE_PAIRS = 32**, so the culling code will never consider more than 32 lights total.  I can easily change the limit up to 256, yielding this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f945162-1664-4257-93e2-18f9c8313c61)

That isn't super efficient, however...it might be best to move the "keep the best N lights" into the culling code, and directly link them there as well.

Note that a fix for this might also fix [Issue 67508](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/67508)

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP attached below.  Go to the 3D preview.  You should see something like the following:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96aaf5cb-1957-4b5c-b372-9d8098fe1326)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[incorrect_lights_per_mesh.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20560152/incorrect_lights_per_mesh.zip)

summary: Fix scene reload causing segfaults when non-existent node is currently selected

description.text: Errors in the way we were handling deselecting nodes were causing segfaults when the currently edited scene was reloaded. When reloading a scene, the currently selected node may not exist. This is supposed to be handled by EditorSelection::update(), which emits a signal that calls SceneTreeDock::_selection_changed(), which calls SceneTreeDock::_push_item, which calls EditorNode::get_singleton()->push_node_item. However, the problem is thus: `SceneTreeDock::_push_item` always calls `push_node_item` for nullptr objects, but this ends up being useless, because `EditorNode::push_node_item` was checking to see if the currently edited object in the inspector was a Node; if the inspector object is null, then it won't be a node, so nothing happens, and the NodeDock and associated docks don't get their selection updated. As a result, interacting with any of the NodeDocks besides the SceneTreeDock after a scene reload with a removed node selected can cause a crash. The easiest way to cause this is by interacting with the Node menu and double clicking on one of the signals. Checking if the currently edited node in the inspector is null and calling `push_item` if so fixes the issue.

summary: Macos crash with multiple run instances when closing the primary instance w/ floating workspace enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on 4.5.dev5; since `f658161619fab52547c408b6d07ecd2ff4766f7e`

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev.mono (64b09905c) - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 (Apple8) - Apple M2 (8 threads)

### Issue description

By default, projects are now run in an "embedded" mode with a toolbar attached to the main run instance <https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/editor/plugins/game_view_plugin.cpp#L736-L737>

However, if multiple run instances are enabled and the main run instance is closed before the other instance(s), the following crash occurs:

```
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = EXC_BAD_ACCESS (code=1, address=0xa00)
  * frame #0: 0x000000010391316c godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`Ref<RemoteDebuggerPeer>::is_valid(this=0x0000000000000a00) const at ref_counted.h:193:40
    frame #1: 0x000000010391311c godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`ScriptEditorDebugger::is_session_active(this=0x0000000000000000) at script_editor_debugger.h:323:41
    frame #2: 0x000000010394f014 godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`ScriptEditorDebugger::_put_msg(this=0x0000000000000000, p_message=0x000000016fdfb6b8, p_data=0x000000016fdfb6b0, p_
thread_id=1) at script_editor_debugger.cpp:75:6
    frame #3: 0x00000001039642d0 godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`ScriptEditorDebugger::send_message(this=0x0000000000000000, p_message=0x000000016fdfb6b8, p_args=0x000000016fdfb6b0
) at script_editor_debugger.cpp:1941:2
    frame #4: 0x000000010049f170 godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`LayerHost::gui_input(this=0x0000000147ef3210, p_event=0x000000016fdfb8b0) at embedded_process_macos.mm:285:20
    frame #5: 0x0000000104822b90 godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64.mono`Control::_call_gui_input(this=0x0000000147ef3210, p_event=0x000000016fdfb8b0) at control.cpp:1865:2
```

It looks like the `ScriptEditorDebugger` instance is null so potentially just a null check needs to be added to `embedded_process_macos.mm:285` but I'm not sure yet if this is a race or if there is other cleanup missing.

Separately, it didn't seem worth making an issue for but the embedded game workspace controls allow for embedding and floating to be each turned off but that gets stored in project metadata and is not actually a project setting, so it is unnecessarily weird to re-enable.

### Steps to reproduce

Make sure the `run/window_placement/game_embed_mode` editor setting is either set to default or set to force the game workspace to be floating.

On a new project, create a blank scene and enable 2 run instances, run the project, and then close the "main" instance (the one with the workspace controls).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A; blank project works fine and the issue is dependent on editor settings

summary: Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic filtering causing blurry textures in Godot 4 for old Intel integrated graphics

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested back to 4.0.4 Stable, but I've narrowed the issue down to affecting my two 2018-era Intel NUCs with their old integrated graphics.  One runs Debian 11.11 and the other runs Windows 10.

- I do not get the issue on my circa 2021 Dell G15 gaming laptop running Windows 10 (Dell G15 5515 Gaming Laptop AMD Ryzen 7 5800H 16GB RAM RTX 3060 6GB)
- I do not get the issue if I switch to Compatibility rendering
- I do not get the issue if I switch back to Godot 3.6 on the affected Intel boxes

### System information

Windows 10/Debian 11, 2018-era beloved potatoes: Intel NUC6/7, integrated graphics (e.g. Pentium J5005, UHD Graphics 605)

### Issue description

I'm getting filtered/blurry textures when switching on Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic filtering on textures.  This is of course the opposite of what anisotropic filtering does.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eea16019-9271-4c32-a56b-b46ebc38f574)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09770694-6f21-45b3-a3a5-b0b86873e844)

On my affected boxes, switching Godot 4 to use Compatibility resolves the issue and the Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic textures become crisp/unfiltered again.

Given all the above, I'm guessing old Intel integrated GPUs don't play nice with Vulkan when it comes to anisotropic filtering, since this wasn't a problem for me on Godot 3/OpenGL using the same devices.  Probably nothing to be done about this, as older hardware will inevitably fall out of support over time.  So just logging this here as a curiosity in case others running Godot on their beloved ancient Intel potatoes experience the issue.

### Steps to reproduce

You'll likely only have a chance repro-ing this on an old Intel integrated GPU device five years old or more.

- Start a new project.
- Add a cube.
- Add a standard material to the cube.
- Add the default Godot texture as the albedo texture. Optionally set UV1 to 3,2,1 for uniform faces.
- In the material Sampling setting, set it to Nearest Mipmap.  Observe the texture is now crisp and unfiltered.
- Now in Sampling setting, set it to Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic.  If your'e affected, observe the texture is now blurred.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

As above.

summary: TextEdit / CodeEdit: set_caret_line does not center the viewport when adjust_viewport is true

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.dev3.official [55fc6e73f]

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot v4.5.dev3.official [55fc6e73f] - Vulkan (Forward+) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090

### Issue description

According to the documentation for TextEdit (and by inheritance, CodeEdit):

> ```gdscript
> void set_caret_line(line: int, adjust_viewport: bool = true, can_be_hidden: bool = true, wrap_index: int = 0, caret_index: int = 0)
>```
> If adjust_viewport is `true`, the viewport will center at the caret position after the move occurs.

Despite `set_caret_line` scrolling to the line number, the viewport is not "centered" as the documentation describes.  The line number will generally be at the top or bottom of the TextEdit or CodeEdit after the move occurs, but it won't be centered.  I'm unsure if this is expected behaviour and "centered" simply refers to the line number being within the bounds of the viewport, however, my expectation was that it would actually place the caret (line number) in the center of the TextEdit or CodeEdit control.

The use-case here is actually a CodeEdit, and I'm using `set_caret_line` to jump to a specific line of code.  It's a bit inconvenient that every time `set_caret_line(line_number, true)` is called, the desired line is almost always at the top of the CodeEdit (hiding all of the code above the caret), or at the bottom of the CodeEdit (hiding all of the code below the caret).

See the attached MRP for an example of this behaviour.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a TextEdit or a CodeEdit
2. Populate it with a bunch of text such that the scrollbar will appear and span multiple viewports worth of scrollable area
3. Call `set_caret_line` to jump to a line that's somewhat far from the currently visible line
4. Observe the behaviour as described above

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[CodeEditBug_v4.5.dev3.official.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20552708/CodeEditBug_v4.5.dev3.official.zip)

summary: Add `WorkerThreadPool.get_caller_group_id`

description.text:
Small addition to the https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107029

Adds and exposes `get_caller_group_id`.

Usage example:

```gdscript
func _task_func(_index):
	# do stuff
	OS.delay_msec(10)
	# call cleanup
	var id = WorkerThreadPool.get_caller_group_id()
	call_deferred("_cleanup_func", id)
	
func _cleanup_func(id):
	# response to single task completion (e.g, update progress bar)
	if WorkerThreadPool.is_group_task_completed(id):
		WorkerThreadPool.wait_for_group_task_completion(id)
		print("task group %d is done" % id)

func _ready() -> void:
	WorkerThreadPool.add_group_task(_task_func, 200, -1, false, "test group")
```

summary: Incomplete export template install is not reported

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

I have a Windows machine where the C drive is almost completely full. I installed the export templates from Export Template Manager and it looked successful. Then, I exported my project. But the resulting .exe file was an empty file with size of 0 bytes.

Looking into Godot/export_templates/4.4.1.stable, I noticed that most files were empty. E.g., windows_release_*.exe were all 0 size files. I fixed it by uninstalling the export template, making more room on my C drive, and then installing them again.

However, I think Export Template Manager should report it to me when the install was incomplete and give me a reason as well. In this case, not enough room on the disk.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a C drive with little free space (e.g., 1GB)
2. Install export templates from the Export Template Manager
3. Export your project
4. See that the .exe size is 0
5. See that some .exe files in the installed export templates are also empty

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

An exportable project (I believe it just has to have a root node).
